name: Heka
description: Heka is the divine embodiment of magic in Egyptian mythology. He is not merely a god of magic, but rather the very essence of magical power that permeates the universe and sustains existence. Heka predates the creation of the world, existing as a fundamental force harnessed by the gods to create and maintain order (Ma'at). He is often depicted as a man holding two snakes, symbolizing his control over both benevolent and malevolent forces. As the power that animates the gods and the world, Heka is considered to be more fundamental than even the creator gods themselves. He is the source of their power, the means by which they enact their will and maintain the cosmic balance. Heka's influence extends to healing, protection, and the ability to manipulate reality. While not always actively worshipped in temples dedicated solely to him, Heka was invoked in nearly every religious ritual and magical practice. His presence was essential for the success of any undertaking, ensuring the efficacy of spells and the power of the gods. He is the life force itself, the breath that sustains all beings, and the ultimate source of magical energy within the Egyptian cosmos. Heka's power is subtle yet pervasive, a fundamental principle that underpins the entire Egyptian worldview. He ensures the continued functionality of the universe and the power of the pharaoh.
subtitle: The Personification of Magic

keyMyths.title: Heka's Primordial Existence
keyMyths.description: Heka existed before creation, a fundamental force used by the gods. This pre-existence highlights his importance to the cosmos.

keyMyths.title: Heka and Healing
keyMyths.description: Heka's power was invoked in healing rituals to restore health. He represented the magic that could mend the body and spirit.

keyMyths.title: Heka and Protection
keyMyths.description: Heka's magic offered protection against evil spirits and chaos. He was a powerful force for warding off danger.

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
